Caption: Thermal analysis and near-equilibrium solidification characteristics of ternary Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi immiscible alloy: (a) alloy location in Cu-Sn-Bi phase diagram [21], (b) DTA curves, (c) solidification morphology of DTA alloy sample (The inserts are the enlarged view of Cu3Sn-rich and Bi-rich zones respectively), (d) XRD patterns of alloy samples solidified with various ultrasonic amplitudes.
Microstructural profiles of ternary Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi alloy solidified under different ultrasonic conditions: (a) A 0 = 0 μm, (b) A 0 = 14.5 μm, (c) A 0 = 20.3 μm, (d) A 0 = 24 μm.

Phase separation characteristics of ternary Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi immiscible alloy: (a) uniformity degree versus ultrasonic amplitudes, (b-c) solute distribution of Cu, Sn and Bi elements under the ultrasonic amplitude of 0 and 24 μm, (d) size distribution of secondary (Bi) particles versus ultrasonic amplitude.

The calculated Stokes motion and Marangoni migration of (Bi) particles, and flow velocity in ternary Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi alloy melt: (a) Stokes and Marangoni motion velocities versus droplet radius, (b) the maximum and average acoustic streaming velocities.

Phase separation mechanisms of ternary Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi immiscible alloy without and with ultrasound: (a) homogeneous alloy melt, (b) static, (c) with ultrasound. Here, V s, V m and V a denote the Stokes motion, Marangoni migration and the acoustic streaming, respectively.

Electrochemical corrosion resistance of Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi immiscible alloy in 3.5% NaCl solution: (a) polarization curves, (b) corrosion current density and area fraction of (Bi) phase, (c-d) corrosion morphologies of alloy samples solidified with ultrasonic amplitudes of 0 and 24 μm.

Mechanical properties of Cu-32%Sn-20%Bi immiscible alloy after ultrasonic treatment: (a) the average microhardness along the vertical direction, (b) average friction coefficient and abrasion loss versus ultrasonic amplitude.
